verses.3: Shall vain words have an end?
verses.4: I also could speak as you do.
verses.5: but I would strengthen you with my mouth.
verses.6: “Though I speak, my grief is not subsided.
verses.7: But now, God, you have surely worn me out.
verses.8: You have shriveled me up. This is a witness against me.
verses.9: He has torn me in his wrath and persecuted me.
verses.10: They have gaped on me with their mouth.
verses.11: God delivers me to the ungodly,
verses.12: I was at ease, and he broke me apart.
verses.14: He breaks me with breach on breach.
verses.15: I have sewed sackcloth on my skin,
verses.16: My face is red with weeping.
verses.17: although there is no violence in my hands,
verses.19: Even now, behold, my witness is in heaven.
verses.21: that he would maintain the right of a man with God,
verses.22: For when a few years have come,
